title: cicer bijugum
intro: Cicer bijugum, also known as the two-leaved bishop's weed or ajwain, is a plant species native to India and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Apiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Cicer bijugum has been used to treat various health issues such as indigestion, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, offering benefits like digestive aid, relief from nausea, and anti-asthmatic properties. Herbal preparations of Cicer bijugum include oil and powder extracts, which can be used in cooking or taken as supplements to harness its medicinal benefits.
